title: In-process async write queue in the PWA backend
userStory: As an operator, I want a small write queue inside the PWA so that the capture endpoint can return in <100ms even when extraction takes seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: New file pwa/src/lib/server/queue.ts: in-memory FIFO with enqueue(payload) returning a synthetic queue_id immediately
Worker loop (default 1s tick, configurable) drains the queue by calling graphiti add_episode per payload
On worker error: log to stderr (per Phase 2 US-006 pattern); retry once or drop (decided by operational experience)
On graceful shutdown (SIGTERM): drain remaining items before exit
No persistent spool. Acceptable failure mode: a PWA crash loses in-flight items. If/when this becomes a real complaint, add a JSON-lines spool file at that point — not before
Verify: enqueue a payload; queue_id returned in <10ms; episode visible via search_brain within ~3s
